name: Catfish Bienville
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw frozen fish according to package directions.
2. Prepare Bienville Sauce; keep warm.
3. Place fillets on a well-greased rack in the broiler pan.
4. Combine butter, lemon juice, salt, and white pepper; brush fillets with the butter mixture.
5. Broil 6 inches from heat for 8 minutes, or until fish flakes easily when tested with a fork.
6. Remove fish to a warm serving platter; spoon Bienville Sauce over fillets.
7. Garnish with parsley and cherry tomato if desired.
BIENVILLE SAUCE
1. In a medium skillet, cook bacon and chopped green onions (with tops) until the bacon is lightly browned.
2. Add butter and flour; cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture is smooth (do not brown the flour).
3. Gradually add milk while making a smooth paste; cook over low heat until thickened and bubbly.
4. Add cheese in small pieces; continue cooking over low heat until the cheese melts.
5. Stir in sherry, cooked shrimp, white pepper, butter, all-purpose flour, hot pepper sauce, and Worcestershire sauce.
